name: Find and fix a race condition in concurrent counter
task: The file /tmp/eval_race/counter.py contains a Counter class with a race condition: increment() reads self.value into a temporary, yields the scheduler, then writes the temporary + 1 back, so when multiple threads call increment() concurrently the final value is less than expected. Fix the Counter class so that 10 threads each calling increment() 2000 times produces a final value of EXACTLY 20000. Do not change the public API (the value attribute, the increment method, the run function) or the signatures. You MAY add internal synchronization (e.g. a lock) so the read-modify-write becomes atomic.

validation.script: import importlib.util, threading; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_race/counter.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); c = mod.Counter(); threads = [threading.Thread(target=mod.run, args=(c, 2000)) for _ in range(10)]; [t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]; assert c.value == 20000, f'race not fixed: got {c.value}, expected 20000'; print('OK')
